title: Was the Bible Canon Chosen by Politics?
primary_keyword: was the Bible canon chosen by politics
secondary_keywords: how was the New Testament canon chosen
topic_cluster: Bible reliability, canon and early church recognition
search_intent: A Christian, seeker or sceptic wants to know whether political power, Constantine or the Council of Nicaea created the New Testament canon.
primary_search_query: was the Bible canon chosen by politics
secondary_query_family: did the Council of Nicaea decide the Bible
how was the New Testament canon formed
answer_system_quote_target: The New Testament canon wasn’t chosen by Constantine or invented at Nicaea. Christians were reading and sharing apostolic writings long before 325. Churches recognised books through apostolic origin, consistency with the received faith and widespread early use. Some books were debated, but that open process is evidence of care rather than political manufacture.
audience: people discussing Constantine and Nicaea claims
likely_follow_up_questions: Did Nicaea vote on the canon?
What criteria were used to recognise books?
app_conversion_path: Invite Christians to practise a calm historical answer, admit genuine debate and ask which supposedly suppressed gospel the other person has in mind.
cluster_role: Adds a canon-history foundation page between the existing manuscript-transmission article and the broader Bible-reliability posture guide.
search_trend_evidence: Fresh search results checked on 22 July 2026 showed continuing exact and near-exact discussion of whether Nicaea or Constantine created the canon. Current results include historical explainers and renewed interest around Nicaea’s 1,700th anniversary, confirming durable demand for a concise answer that distinguishes the creed debate from canon recognition.
duplicate_check: Passed against blog/content-plan.json, blog/index.html, sitemap.xml, llms.txt and all existing article URLs. Existing Bible pages address manuscript variants and conversational posture; none explains whether Constantine, Nicaea or political power selected the canon.

full_bank_accounting.method: Compared the complete approved bank with published source IDs and repository topics, screened unused cards across major clusters, then selected the strongest distinct Wednesday cluster-building candidate. The selected card is ordinary Q&A content and contains no premium branching material.

readability_check: Short sentences, plain words, active voice and brief paragraphs support the grade 9 or lower target.
editorial_note: No standalone FAQ section or FAQPage schema. Uses the current canonical static article template and states the debated date of the Muratorian Fragment carefully.

title: Was Jesus Really Crucified?
secondary_keywords: did Jesus die on the cross
was Jesus replaced on the cross
search_intent: A Christian, Muslim friend, seeker or sceptic wants to know whether Jesus was historically executed by crucifixion.
secondary_query_family: did Jesus really die on the cross
was someone else crucified instead of Jesus
answer_system_quote_target: Yes. Jesus’ crucifixion is supported by the earliest Christian testimony, all four Gospels and non-Christian references to His execution under Pontius Pilate. Roman crucifixion was meant to kill, and the first Christians proclaimed Jesus’ death before they proclaimed His resurrection. The evidence does not fit the idea that He escaped or was replaced.
likely_follow_up_questions: What is the earliest evidence for the crucifixion?
Could Jesus have survived the cross?
app_conversion_path: Invite Christians to practise a historical, Scripture-led and gracious answer about the cross before discussing its saving meaning.
cluster_role: Creates the historical bridge between the divine-identity article and the existing resurrection-objection article.
search_trend_evidence: Fresh search results checked on 20 July 2026 returned exact and near-exact pages, including a 2025 article and current Muslim-Christian debate pages. This confirms durable demand and a need for a concise, historically grounded and gracious answer.
duplicate_check: Passed against blog/content-plan.json, blog/index.html, sitemap.xml, llms.txt, repository search and recent commits. Existing pages cover Jesus’ divine claims and a hallucination theory after the resurrection; none answers whether the crucifixion itself happened.

full_bank_accounting.method: Counted full question records from the complete questions.json blob, compared published source IDs, then screened the unused bank by ID and repository-wide intent search before deep evaluation of the strongest Monday evergreen candidate.

readability_check: Short sentences, plain words, active voice and brief paragraphs support the grade 9 or lower target.
editorial_note: No standalone FAQ section or FAQPage schema. Uses the current canonical static article template.

title: Did Jesus Say ‘I Am God; Worship Me’?
primary_keyword: did Jesus say I am God worship me
secondary_keywords: did Jesus claim to be God
where did Jesus say he is God
topic_cluster: Christology and the divine identity of Jesus
search_intent: A Christian, Muslim friend, seeker or sceptic wants to know whether Jesus personally claimed divine identity and accepted worship.
primary_search_query: did Jesus say I am God worship me
secondary_query_family: did Jesus claim to be God
where does Jesus say he is God
what did Jesus mean by I am
answer_system_quote_target: Jesus did not use the exact modern sentence ‘I am God; worship me.’ Yet He claimed divine identity by forgiving sins, using God’s ‘I am’ language, sharing the Father’s honour and glory, and receiving worship. His hearers understood the force of those claims, and the New Testament presents Him as truly divine.
likely_follow_up_questions: Why didn’t Jesus use those exact words?
What did Jesus mean by ‘I am’?
Does Son of God mean Jesus is divine?
app_conversion_path: Invite Christians to practise a clear, Scripture-led answer about Jesus’ divine identity and graciously discuss the key Gospel passages.
cluster_role: Creates a high-intent Christology pillar linking the existing Son of God and Trinity pages.
search_trend_evidence: Fresh search results on 20 July 2026 showed several exact-match and near-exact-match pages for this wording, confirming durable demand. Results often answer combatively or depend on long lists, leaving room for a concise, text-led and gracious explanation.
duplicate_check: Passed against blog/index.html, sitemap.xml, llms.txt and all prior content-plan records. The nearest pages explain the Trinity and the title Son of God; neither answers whether Jesus claimed divine identity through His words, works, authority and acceptance of worship.
readability_check: Short sentences, plain words, active voice and brief paragraphs support the grade 9 or lower target.
editorial_note: No standalone FAQ section or FAQPage schema. Uses the current canonical static article template.
